Administration (20)  [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") (17)  [Liberal Democrats](/wiki/Liberal_Democrats_(UK) "Liberal Democrats (UK)") (3)  Other parties (35)  [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") (19)  [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") (7)  [Reform UK](/wiki/Reform_UK "Reform UK") (5)  [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") (4)

is a [local government district](/wiki/Non-metropolitan_district "Non-metropolitan district") with [borough status](/wiki/Borough_status_in_the_United_Kingdom "Borough status in the United Kingdom") in [Norfolk](/wiki/Norfolk "Norfolk"), England. The district also includes the towns of [Downham Market](/wiki/Downham_Market "Downham Market") and [Hunstanton](/wiki/Hunstanton "Hunstanton"), along with numerous villages and surrounding rural areas. The population of the district at the 2021 census was 154,325.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

**King's Lynn and West Norfolk** is a [local government district](/wiki/Non-metropolitan_district "Non-metropolitan district") with [borough status](/wiki/Borough_status_in_the_United_Kingdom "Borough status in the United Kingdom") in [Norfolk](/wiki/Norfolk "Norfolk"), England. Its council is based in the town of [King's Lynn](/wiki/King%27s_Lynn "King's Lynn"). The district also includes the towns of [Downham Market](/wiki/Downham_Market "Downham Market") and [Hunstanton](/wiki/Hunstanton "Hunstanton"), along with numerous villages and surrounding rural areas. The population of the district at the 2021 census was 154,325.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Part of the borough lies within the [Norfolk Coast Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty](/wiki/Norfolk_Coast_AONB "Norfolk Coast AONB"). The borough lies on the coast, facing both [The Wash](/wiki/The_Wash "The Wash") to the west and the [North Sea](/wiki/North_Sea "North Sea") to the north. The neighbouring districts are [North Norfolk](/wiki/North_Norfolk "North Norfolk"), [Breckland](/wiki/Breckland_District "Breckland District"), [West Suffolk](/wiki/West_Suffolk_District "West Suffolk District"), [East Cambridgeshire](/wiki/East_Cambridgeshire "East Cambridgeshire"), [Fenland](/wiki/Fenland_District "Fenland District") and [South Holland](/wiki/South_Holland_District "South Holland District").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The district was created on 1 April 1974 under the [Local Government Act 1972](/wiki/Local_Government_Act_1972 "Local Government Act 1972"), covering seven former districts which were all abolished at the same time:[[3]](#cite_note-3)

* [Docking Rural District](/wiki/Docking_Rural_District "Docking Rural District")
* [Downham Market](/wiki/Downham_Market "Downham Market") [Urban District](/wiki/Urban_district_(England_and_Wales) "Urban district (England and Wales)")
* [Downham Rural District](/wiki/Downham_Rural_District "Downham Rural District")
* [Freebridge Lynn Rural District](/wiki/Freebridge_Lynn_Rural_District "Freebridge Lynn Rural District")
* [Hunstanton](/wiki/Hunstanton "Hunstanton") Urban District
* [King's Lynn](/wiki/King%27s_Lynn "King's Lynn") [Municipal Borough](/wiki/Municipal_Borough "Municipal Borough")
* [Marshland Rural District](/wiki/Marshland_Rural_District "Marshland Rural District")

The new district was initially named "West Norfolk" reflecting its position in the wider county.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The district was awarded borough status on 30 January 1981, allowing the chair of the council to take the title of mayor. The name of the borough was changed later that year, becoming "King's Lynn and West Norfolk" with effect from 14 May 1981.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Governance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Governance")]

The Borough Council of King's Lynn and West Norfolk provides [district-level](/wiki/Non-metropolitan_district "Non-metropolitan district") services. [County-level](/wiki/Non-metropolitan_county "Non-metropolitan county") services are provided by [Norfolk County Council](/wiki/Norfolk_County_Council "Norfolk County Council"). Much of the borough is also covered by [civil parishes](/wiki/Civil_parish "Civil parish"), which form a third tier of local government.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-electionmaps-9)

### Political control

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Political control")]

The council has been under [no overall control](/wiki/No_overall_control "No overall control") since the [2023 election](/wiki/2023_King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk_Borough_Council_election "2023 King's Lynn and West Norfolk Borough Council election"), being run by a coalition of most of the independent councillors, the Liberal Democrats and the Greens with informal support from Labour.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The first election to the council was held in 1973, initially operating as a shadow authority alongside the outgoing authorities until the new arrangements took effect on 1 April 1974. Political control of the council since 1974 has been as follows:[[11]](#cite_note-compositions-11)[[12]](#cite_note-hold-12)

| Party in control | | Years |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | [No overall control](/wiki/No_overall_control "No overall control") | 1974–1976 |
|  | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") | 1976–1991 |
|  | [No overall control](/wiki/No_overall_control "No overall control") | 1991–1995 |
|  | [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") | 1995–1999 |
|  | [No overall control](/wiki/No_overall_control "No overall control") | 1999–2003 |
|  | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") | 2003–2023 |
|  | [No overall control](/wiki/No_overall_control "No overall control") | 2023–present |

### Leadership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Leadership")]

The role of mayor is largely ceremonial in King's Lynn and West Norfolk, with political leadership instead provided by the [leader of the council](/wiki/Leader_of_the_council "Leader of the council"). The leaders since 2007 have been:

| Councillor | Party | | From | To |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Nick Daubney[[13]](#cite_note-13) |  | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") | 2007 | 12 May 2016 |
| Brian Long[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) |  | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") | 12 May 2016 | 20 May 2021 |
| Stuart Dark[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17) |  | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") | 20 May 2021 | May 2023 |
| Terry Parish[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) |  | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") | 18 May 2023 | May 2024 |
| Alistair Beale[[20]](#cite_note-20) |  | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") | 16 May 2024 |  |

### Composition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Composition")]

Following the [2023 election](/wiki/2023_King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk_Borough_Council_election "2023 King's Lynn and West Norfolk Borough Council election"),[[21]](#cite_note-21) and subsequent by-elections and changes of allegiance up to March 2026, the composition of the council was:[[22]](#cite_note-Thorncliffe-22)

| Party | | Councillors |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") | 19 |
|  | [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") | 7 |
|  | [Liberal Democrats](/wiki/Liberal_Democrats_(UK) "Liberal Democrats (UK)") | 3 |
|  | [Reform UK](/wiki/Reform_UK "Reform UK") | 5 |
|  | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") | 21 |
| Total | | 55 |

Of the 22 independent councillors, 16 sit together as the 'Independent Partnership', which forms the council's administration with the Liberal Democrats and Green Party. Another two form the 'Progressive Group', and the other four do not belong to a group.[[23]](#cite_note-23) The next election is due in 2027.[[22]](#cite_note-Thorncliffe-22)

### Elections

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Elections")]

See also: [King's Lynn and West Norfolk Borough Council elections](/wiki/King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk_Borough_Council_elections "King's Lynn and West Norfolk Borough Council elections")

Since the last boundary changes in 2019 the council has comprised 55 [councillors](/wiki/Councillor "Councillor") representing 35 [wards](/wiki/Wards_and_electoral_divisions_of_the_United_Kingdom "Wards and electoral divisions of the United Kingdom"), with each ward electing one, two or three councillors. Elections are held every four years.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

UK Youth Parliament

Although the [UK Youth Parliament](/wiki/UK_Youth_Parliament "UK Youth Parliament") is an apolitical organisation, the elections are run in a way similar to that of the Local Elections. The votes come from 11 to 18 year olds and are combined to make the decision of the next, 2 year [Member of Youth Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Youth_Parliament "Member of Youth Parliament"). The elections are run at different times across the country with King's Lynn and West Norfolk's typically being in early Spring and bi-annually.

### Premises

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Premises")]

The council generally meets at the [Town Hall](/wiki/King%27s_Lynn_Guildhall "King's Lynn Guildhall") on the Saturday Market Place in King's Lynn, parts of which date back to the 1420s, having replaced an earlier guildhall on the site which burnt down in 1421.[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26) The council's main offices are at King's Court, on Chapel Street in King's Lynn, which had been built as speculative offices in 1975, initially being called Aspen House.[[27]](#cite_note-27) The council bought the building in 1981.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/33/The_seafront_at_Hunstanton_-_geograph.org.uk_-_2793449.jpg/250px-The_seafront_at_Hunstanton_-_geograph.org.uk_-_2793449.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_seafront_at_Hunstanton_-_geograph.org.uk_-_2793449.jpg)

[Hunstanton](/wiki/Hunstanton "Hunstanton"), one of the coastal towns on the Norfolk Coast.

The district comprises the urban area of King's Lynn itself, together with 102 surrounding parishes. At the time of the [2001 census](/wiki/United_Kingdom_Census_2001 "United Kingdom Census 2001"), the district had an area of [1,473 km2](/wiki/Orders_of_magnitude_(area)#108_to_1014_square_metres "Orders of magnitude (area)"), of which 28 km2 was in the urban area and 1,445 km2 in the surrounding parishes. The district had a population of 135,345 in 58,338 households, with 34,564 in 15,285 households living in the urban area, whilst 100,781 people in 43,053 households lived in the surrounding parishes.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

## Towns and parishes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Towns and parishes")]

See also: [List of civil parishes in Norfolk](/wiki/List_of_civil_parishes_in_Norfolk "List of civil parishes in Norfolk")

The main part of the urban area of King's Lynn (roughly corresponding to the pre-1974 borough of King's Lynn) is an [unparished area](/wiki/Unparished_area "Unparished area").[[9]](#cite_note-electionmaps-9) The remainder of the district is covered by [civil parishes](/wiki/Civil_parish "Civil parish"). The parish councils for Downham Market and Hunstanton have declared their parishes to be towns, allowing them to take the style "town council". The parishes are:[[30]](#cite_note-30)

## Arms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=King%27s_Lynn_and_West_Norfolk&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Arms")]

Coat of arms of King's Lynn and West Norfolk

|  |
| --- |
| Crest  Upon a wreath Or and Azure upon a bollard Sable roped Or a seagull Proper gorged with a coronet and holding in the dexter claw a cross botonny fitchy Or.  Escutcheon  Per chevron Azure and Or three dragons' heads erect and erased each transfixed through the mouth by a cross botonny fitchy all within a bordure per chevron counter-changed.  Supporters  On either side a sea lion Or supporting with the exterior leg an ostrich feather Argent.[[31]](#cite_note-31)  Badge  A sea lion Or within a garland of oakleaves fructed Proper. |
